changelog:
Highly customizable API keys.

Every key now carries up to four orthogonal kinds of limits, plus richer expiry controls:

  Limit dimensions
  - Per-minute request rate     (burst protection — req/min)
  - Per-hour request rate       (coarse burst control — req/hour)
  - Periodic budget             (req / tokens / credits — resets on the chosen period)
  - Lifetime caps               (req / tokens / credits — auto-disables key when reached)

  Reset windows for the periodic budget
  - daily / weekly (Mon) / monthly (1st)
  - any IANA timezone (Asia/Singapore, Europe/Berlin, America/New_York etc.)

  Expiry modes (any combination)
  - Absolute expiry  (datetime-local picker — calendar widget for any future date)
  - Lazy expiry      (counts down N days from first successful call; an unused key never expires)

All limits are independent. Zero on any dimension means unlimited on that dimension. Order of checks per request: disabled -> absolute expiry -> lazy expiry -> model whitelist -> per-minute -> per-hour -> periodic -> lifetime. Lifetime breaches auto-disable the key.

Dashboard create / edit dialog now exposes every dimension grouped into 5 sections (Basic / Periodic / Burst / Lifetime / Expiry). Card display shows live counters for each active limit as compact pill badges.

Backend extensions: APIKey struct + UpdateAPIKeyOptions struct; ConsumeAPIKey rewritten with the 8-step ordered checks; admin PUT /admin/api/apikeys/{id} accepts every new field as nullable pointer.

A12: unify api keys (legacy single key auto-migrates to multi-key list).
A11: WebSocket transport for /v1/responses (Codex experimental).
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
